content.verdict: Galley Bay sits along three-quarters of a mile of soft white sand on Antigua's western coast, with a bird sanctuary lagoon at its back and the open sea at its front. Adults-only, all-inclusive, and consistently among the Caribbean's best-value adult-only properties, it offers Gauguin Cottages on the lagoon, Beachfront Premium Suites on the sand, and a relaxed barefoot atmosphere that suits couples seeking simplicity.
content.best_room: The Beachfront Premium Suites sit directly on the sand — wood interiors, four-poster beds, and a few steps from the water. The Gauguin Cottages are individual thatched-roof cottages built on stilts on the lagoon side, each with private outdoor showers and a Polynesian aesthetic. For couples wanting beachfront, Premium Suites are unbeatable; for unique character, the Gauguin Cottages are the romantic choice.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Beach Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from V.C. Bird International (25 minutes to Five Islands on the western coast). Galley Bay's all-inclusive includes premium spirits, wines, and most water sports — the welcome drink and tour set the tone. First dinner at Sea Grape, the open-air beachfront restaurant, with the sunset directly in front.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Day & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Galley Bay's three-quarter-mile beach is one of Antigua's longest — the western exposure means consistent sunsets and calm afternoon water. Morning paddleboard, afternoon at the Indulge Spa for a couples' Antiguan-salt treatment, and sunset cocktails at the beach bar with a steel-pan player.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: A drive south across the island (45 minutes) to Nelson's Dockyard, the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard. Lunch at one of the harbourside restaurants among the restored 18th-century buildings, and an afternoon walking through the historic site.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive up to Shirley Heights, the 18th-century military lookout above English Harbour, for sunset. The panorama down to Falmouth Harbour's yachts is Antigua's most photographed view. Sunday evenings host the legendary steel band sunset gathering with barbecue.

itinerary_7_nights.title: 365 Beaches Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private driver day exploring Antigua's 365 beaches: Half Moon Bay on the Atlantic side (surf), Pink Sand Beach, Ffryes Bay (beach bars), and Darkwood Beach. Return to Galley Bay for dinner at Ismay's, the resort's main fine-dining restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & Snorkelling
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat trip to Stingray City off Antigua's north coast — a shallow sandbar where southern stingrays glide in waist-deep water. The trip includes a snorkel stop at offshore reefs. Afternoon at the bay's calmer end for a kayak paddle through the lagoon bird sanctuary.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Beach Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last swim at Galley Bay, breakfast on the suite terrace, and a 25-minute road transfer back to V.C. Bird International. Most international flights depart in the afternoon, allowing a full final morning at the bay.

caveats: Galley Bay is genuinely barefoot-casual — couples expecting white-glove luxury service of Jumby Bay or Hermitage Bay calibre will find the resort more relaxed in style.
The lagoon at the back of the property is a bird sanctuary, beautiful but with occasional mosquitoes at dusk — repellent is wise.
Some of the older Beach Cottages (non-Premium) are showing age compared to the renovated Premium category — book Premium specifically for the best experience.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Galley Bay is one of Antigua's best honeymoon values — adults-only, all-inclusive, with a stunning long beach and the unique Gauguin Cottages on the lagoon. The relaxed barefoot atmosphere suits couples who want beach and simplicity over high-formality luxury.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is high season — dry, sunny, breezy. February-March is peak. May, June, and November offer lower rates. September-October is hurricane season; the resort sometimes closes for refurbishment.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: For Christmas, New Year, and February-March, book 6-9 months ahead. Beachfront Premium Suites and Gauguin Cottages sell out first. Shoulder season offers good availability with 2-4 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — Galley Bay is strictly adults-only (18+). This is one of its core appeals for honeymooners seeking a relaxed adult Caribbean week without children.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Beachfront Premium Suites for direct beach access; Gauguin Cottages for unique Polynesian-inspired character on the lagoon. Both categories are honeymoon-appropriate. Avoid the older non-Premium Beach Cottages if budget allows.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU). Galley Bay is 25 minutes by road on the western coast at Five Islands — the resort arranges private transfers. Direct flights serve Antigua from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, Toronto, and major European hubs.
